rules.reason: Used ADX_14 > 25 to confirm established trend. Entry requires price above/below SMA_20 (trend direction) and close in direction of trend (vs prev_close). Pullback identification uses prev_high/low as recent swing points. Exit triggers on trend structure break (close below/above recent pullback extremes or SMA_20 violation). CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction handled via separate LONG and SHORT entry/exit conditions.

Signal describes trend termination via failure patterns (springs/upthrusts) with momentum weakness. Entry conditions translated to: price breaking prior extreme with volume surge and momentum divergence (RSI moving away from overbought/oversold). Exit conditions use momentum reversal (MACD crossover) and RSI extremes indicating false breakout failure. Spring/upthrust patterns themselves are not directly available as indicators, so entry uses price + volume + RSI as proxies for momentum weakness at extremes. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction preserved as signal applies to both trend directions.

Signal is context-dependent (LONG in uptrends, SHORT in downtrends). Entry conditions check MA relationship (200MA below/above 20MA) AND price proximity to 200MA as dynamic support/resistance. Exit conditions trigger when price breaches the 200MA line. Multi-timeframe monitoring (5m, 15m, 60m) should be applied at backtest execution level. Used sma_200 and sma_20 as specified indicators.

Entry conditions: (1) lower_wick > body detects rejection candle with long bottom tail; (2) close > sma_50 confirms ongoing uptrend context; (3) close > prev_high ensures next candle is green and takes out previous rejection candle high. Exit condition uses reversal signal (close below previous low) as author provided no exit guidance (AUTHOR_SILENT). Support level detection via price structure is implicit in the uptrend/correction context rather than explicit indicator.

Mapped 'central pivot' to pivot indicator. Entry conditions use pivot level penetration (close < pivot) combined with support rejection (crosses_below s1) to capture reactive players. Exit uses price return toward pivot equilibrium (crosses_above pivot). Signal is context-dependent as it describes reactive mean-reversion within ranging markets. Used conservative stop_loss and hold_days based on intraday/short-term drift mentioned in source.

Classic SMA crossover strategy using sma_10 (short-term) and sma_20 (long-term) as standard parameters. Entry conditions include crossover, price confirmation above/below both MAs, and volume confirmation. Exit on reversal crossover or price breaking shorter SMA. Direction is context-dependent: long when short SMA > long SMA, short when opposite. Applied to TRENDING regimes.

Signal uses SMA(42) and SMA(252). Mapped to closest available indicators: sma_50 (short-period proxy for 42-day) and sma_200 (long-period proxy for 252-day). Uses crosses_above/crosses_below operators for entry/exit crossovers as specified in conditions. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT since signal generates both long and short signals. Target regime set to TRENDING_UP and TRENDING_DOWN per signal's TRENDING target.

Classic SMA crossover strategy (Golden/Death Cross variant). SMA1_period mapped to sma_10 and SMA2_period mapped to sma_20 as standard fast/slow pair. Entry requires crossover AND price confirmation above/below the faster MA. Exit triggered on crossover signal only (reversal condition). Direction is bidirectional: longs on upward cross, shorts on downward cross. Optimal for TRENDING regimes.

Signal uses momentum generation with lag_bars='second but last value' and label_parametrization='right'. Translated momentum using MACD histogram (macd_hist) as the momentum indicator since no explicit 'momentum' indicator is in the allowed list. Entry conditions require positive/negative momentum signal with consistency check approximated by comparing current to previous bar momentum. Exit triggers on momentum reversal (sign change) or zero crossing. Context-dependent direction handles both long and short scenarios based on momentum sign.

Signal describes band breakouts with volatility-adaptive behavior (widest bands in low volatility, narrowest in high volatility). Mapped to Bollinger Bands which exhibit this inverse volatility relationship. Long entry on upper band break, short entry on lower band cross. Since system is always in market, exits are reversals (opposite band crosses). The adaptive smoothing constant behavior mentioned in parameters is inherent to Bollinger Bands calculation.

Signal uses 20-day moving average crossover for both buy and sell signals. Implemented as: BUY when price crosses above sma_20, SELL when price crosses below sma_20. Exit conditions inferred from reversal logic since original source marked 'AUTHOR_SILENT'. Test period: January 2, 1990 through March 28, 1991 on crude oil futures.

Signal uses three moving average hierarchy: faster (ema_10), slower (ema_20), and long-term (ema_50). Entry conditions require price above both shorter MAs with faster below slower. Exit triggers when long-term MA (ema_50) conflicts with shorter MA positioning, representing the neutral condition. Open interest measurement not translatable to available indicators. Parameters mapped using EMA variants as most responsive to Kaufman's adaptive methodology intent.

Signal is context-dependent based on market trend. LONG entries trigger on penetration above previous swing high (close > prev_high). SHORT entries trigger on penetration below previous swing low (close < prev_low). Exits are reversals of entry conditions. Since exact swing high/low calculation method is not specified and not available as a direct indicator, using prev_high/prev_low as the closest proxy for recent swing points. Author provides no explicit exit conditions (AUTHOR_SILENT), so reversal logic is applied as a reasonable alternative.

Signal specifies 40-day moving average period with AUTHOR_SILENT type. Mapped to sma_50 as the closest available period. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction preserved with both long and short entry/exit conditions. Exit conditions implemented as reversals (opposite crossovers) since original exit conditions were AUTHOR_SILENT. Target regime set to TRENDING variants as specified.

Bear put spread is a SHORT directional strategy anticipating downward price movement. Entry condition added: price below 20-day SMA to confirm downward bias. Exit condition set as reversal: price crosses above 20-day SMA. The actual option mechanics (sell lower strike put, buy higher strike put) are structural and handled by the strategy implementation layer, not by price/indicator conditions. Author provided no explicit exit signal (AUTHOR_SILENT), so a logical reversal condition was applied.
